item.content:encoded:
Hugging Face 热门模型日报 2026-06-20

数据来源: Hugging Face Hub | 共 30 个模型 | 生成时间: 2026-06-20 00:28 UTC

好的，作为AI模型生态分析师，以下是基于2026年6月20日数据生成的《Hugging Face 热门模型日报》。

Hugging Face 热门模型日报 | 2026-06-20

今日速览

本周 Hugging Face 榜单呈现三大趋势：顶尖大模型竞争白热化，DeepSeek-V4 Pro 凭借惊人的点赞与下载量登顶，巩固了其在基础模型领域的领先地位；多模态与专用模型成为新宠，NVIDIA 的定位模型、MiniMax 的多模态模型以及众多“代码专用”变体占据了榜单半壁江山；社区驱动的微调与量化生态极其活跃，围绕 Qwen、Gemma、GLM 等家族的 GGUF 量化版本层出不穷，极大降低了开源模型的使用门槛。

热门模型

🧠 语言模型

  1. deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro

    • 作者: deepseek-ai | 点赞: 4,969 | 下载: 3,015,772
    • 说明：DeepSeek 最新旗舰对话模型，凭借强大的综合性能和开源权重，成为本周无可争议的社区焦点。

  2. zai-org/GLM-5.2

    • 作者: zai-org | 点赞: 1,532 | 下载: 11,871
    • 说明：智谱AI的旗舰级MoE模型，代表了非Transformer架构（DS Attention）的前沿探索，获得极高关注。

  3. WeiboAI/VibeThinker-3B

    • 作者: WeiboAI | 点赞: 458 | 下载: 12,148
    • 说明：基于 Qwen2 的3B参数数学推理模型，专注垂域，以轻量化和强逻辑能力吸引开发者。

  4. microsoft/FastContext-1.0-4B-SFT

    • 作者: microsoft | 点赞: 229 | 下载: 1,437
    • 说明：微软推出的长上下文优化小模型，旨在解决长文本处理效率问题，技术探索意义显著。
🎨 多模态与生成

  1. nvidia/LocateAnything-3B

    • 作者: nvidia | 点赞: 2,194 | 下载: 228,669
    • 说明：NVIDIA 推出的通用目标定位模型，结合视觉理解与指令跟随，在交互式AI领域表现惊艳。

  2. google/diffusiongemma-26B-A4B-it

    • 作者: google | 点赞: 1,010 | 下载: 601,208
    • 说明：Google 推出的 MoE 版本视觉语言模型（扩散+Gemma），兼顾理解与生成，下载量巨大。

  3. MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M3

    • 作者: MiniMaxAI | 点赞: 1,133 | 下载: 67,836
    • 说明：MiniMax 的旗舰多模态模型，在图像文本理解与生成任务上表现出色，是商业闭源模型的开源代表。

  4. google/gemma-4-12B-it

    • 作者: google | 点赞: 1,096 | 下载: 1,590,882
    • 说明：Google 最新通用模型，支持“任意到任意”的输入输出（文本、图像、音频等），极具前沿性。

  5. moonshotai/Kimi-K2.7-Code

    • 作者: moonshotai | 点赞: 909 | 下载: 274,865
    • 说明：月之暗面推出的代码专用多模态模型，结合视觉理解与代码能力，下载量持续走高。

  6. nvidia/nemotron-3.5-asr-streaming-0.6b

    • 作者: nvidia | 点赞: 562 | 下载: 18,809
    • 说明：NVIDIA 推出的超低延迟流式语音识别模型，适合实时语音交互场景。

  7. bosonai/higgs-audio-v3-tts-4b

    • 作者: bosonai | 点赞: 492 | 下载: 69,143
    • 说明：基于 Qwen3 的4B参数文本转语音模型，将多模态大模型能力应用于高质量语音合成。

  8. zai-org/SCAIL-2

    • 作者: zai-org | 点赞: 233 | 下载: 0
    • 说明：用于角色动画的图像到视频生成模型，探索基于姿态驱动的视频创作新范式。
🔧 专用模型

  1. yuxinlu1/gemma-4-12B-coder-fable5-composer2.5-v1-GGUF

    • 作者: yuxinlu1 | 点赞: 1,845 | 下载: 268,102
    • 说明：基于 Gemma-4 微调的高性能代码模型，集成多种优化技术，是本周最火的代码专用量化版。

  2. CohereLabs/North-Mini-Code-1.0

    • 作者: CohereLabs | 点赞: 458 | 下载: 17,693
    • 说明：Cohere 推出的轻量代码生成模型，采用 MoE 架构，在代码生成领域表现亮眼。
📦 微调与量化

  1. HauhauCS/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-Uncensored-HauhauCS-Aggressive

    • 作者: HauhauCS | 点赞: 2,006 | 下载: 3,730,978
    • 说明：基于 Qwen3.6 的“无审查”激进版微调模型，下载量惊人，反映了社区对模型自由度的高需求。

  2. DavidAU/Qwen3.6-40B-Claude-4.6-Opus-Deckard-Heretic-Uncensored-Thinking-NEO-CODE-Di-IMatrix-MAX-GGUF

    • 作者: DavidAU | 点赞: 406 | 下载: 588,753
    • 说明：Qwen3.6 的“缝合怪”式微调社区版，集成了“Claude思维链”、“无审查”等流行概念，体现了极客社区的创造力。

  3. Jackrong/Qwopus3.6-27B-Coder-MTP-GGUF

    • 作者: Jackrong | 点赞: 261 | 下载: 148,525
    • 说明：针对代码任务优化的 Qwen3.6 多模态模型，采用 MTP（Multi-Turn Prediction）技术，并提供GGUF量化。

  4. unsloth/GLM-5.2-GGUF、unsloth/diffusiongemma-26B-A4B-it-GGUF 等

    • 作者: unsloth | 点赞: 178-318 | 下载: 8,392-202,867
    • 说明：专业量化机构 unsloth 为几乎所有热门模型（GLM、DiffusionGemma、MiniMax）提供了 GGUF 版本，大幅降低了部署门槛。

生态信号

  • 家族混战，Qwen 生态一枝独秀：从榜单看，Qwen 系列（包括 Qwen3、3.5、3.6）衍生出的微调与量化模型数量远超其他家族，特别是 DavidAU、HauhauCS 等社区创作者围绕其形成了强大的“改装文化”。
  • GGUF 成为标配：几乎每个热门模型都有 unsloth 或社区提供的 GGUF 版本，量化已成为模型发布的必要环节，标志着本地部署和边缘计算需求的爆发。
  • “无审查”与“缝合”趋势：HauhauCS 和 DavidAU 的模型下载量惊人，表明社区对“无审查”和“能力缝合”（如混合思维链）有强烈偏好。
  • 多模态与生成式AI全面开花：榜单中近一半模型涉及图像、音频、视频处理，NVIDIA、Google、MiniMax 等巨头在视觉定位、语音识别、图像生成等领域持续输出前沿开源权重。

值得探索

  1. google/gemma-4-12B-it：作为 Google 首款“任意到任意”统一模型，它代表了多模态AI的未来方向。直接使用或基于它进行微调，都极具研究价值。
  2. nvidia/LocateAnything-3B：如果你对视觉AI代理（如理解屏幕、操控机器人）感兴趣，这个模型将是你实现“万物可指”的最佳起点。
  3. unsloth/diffusiongemma-26B-A4B-it-GGUF：想体验 Google 最前沿的扩散+语言模型，但苦于显存不足？unsloth 的 GGUF 版本让你能在本地流畅运行这个26B的MoE模型，性能与效率的完美平衡点。

本日报由 agents-radar 自动生成。

item.content:encoded:
Hugging Face Trending Models Digest 2026-06-20

Source: Hugging Face Hub | 30 models | Generated: 2026-06-20 00:28 UTC

🤗 Hugging Face Trending Models Digest — 2026-06-20

1. Today's Highlights

This week's Hugging Face ecosystem is dominated by DeepSeek-V4-Pro, which tops the charts with nearly 5,000 weekly likes and over 3 million downloads—a clear signal of the community's appetite for frontier open-weight reasoning models. Google's diffusiongemma-26B-A4B-it also surges, showcasing the growing convergence of diffusion models with multimodal understanding. The quantization ecosystem remains hyperactive, with Unsloth leading the GGUF conversion charge across multiple model families including GLM-5.2, Kimi-K2.7-Code, and MiniMax-M3. Notably, uncensored community fine-tunes continue to proliferate—HauhauCS's Qwen3.6 variant has accumulated over 3.7 million downloads despite sharing only a week ago. Three key trends emerge: the rapid commoditization of 25-40B MoE architectures, the rise of multimodal model families offering "any-to-any" interfaces, and the increasing sophistication of community quantization pipelines that make large models accessible on consumer hardware.

2. Trending Models by Category

🧠 Language Models (LLMs, chat models, instruction-tuned)

  • deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro — deepseek-ai | 4,969 likes, 3,015,772 downloads
    The week's runaway leader: a next-generation conversational MoE model pushing open-weight frontier reasoning, likely the most capable openly available LLM this week.

  • zai-org/GLM-5.2 — zai-org | 1,532 likes, 11,871 downloads
    A new MoE-DSA architecture from ZAI Labs, blending sparse attention with mixture-of-experts for efficient conversational AI.

  • yuxinlu1/gemma-4-12B-coder-fable5-composer2.5-v1-GGUF — yuxinlu1 | 1,845 likes, 268,102 downloads
    A heavily fine-tuned Gemma variant optimized for coding and compositional reasoning, achieving outsized popularity through its GGUF distribution.

  • WeiboAI/VibeThinker-3B — WeiboAI | 458 likes, 12,148 downloads
    A compact 3B model trained with a "chain-of-thought with style" approach, targeting mathematical reasoning at minimal compute footprint.

  • nex-agi/Nex-N2-Pro — nex-agi | 335 likes, 7,507 downloads
    A Qwen3.5-MoE derivative optimized for agentic workflows and tool-use, signaling enterprise interest in LLM-powered agents.

  • HauhauCS/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-Uncensored-HauhauCS-Aggressive — HauhauCS | 2,006 likes, 3,730,978 downloads
    An uncensored, "aggressive" fine-tune of Qwen3.6 MoE with vision capabilities—massively popular despite its niche positioning.

  • DavidAU/Qwen3.6-40B-Claude-4.6-Opus-Deckard-Heretic-Uncensored-Thinking-NEO-CODE-Di-IMatrix-MAX-GGUF — DavidAU | 406 likes, 588,753 downloads
    A maximalist community experiment: blends Claude-style personality with Qwen3.6 MoE, uncensored, GGUF-optimized for coding and reasoning.

  • microsoft/FastContext-1.0-4B-SFT — microsoft | 229 likes, 1,437 downloads
    Microsoft's Explorer SubAgent model: a compact 4B model designed for efficient long-context retrieval and agentic sub-tasks.

🎨 Multimodal & Generation (image, video, audio, text-to-X)

  • MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M3 — MiniMaxAI | 1,133 likes, 67,836 downloads
    A state-of-the-art multimodal vision-language model balancing strong image-text understanding with MoE efficiency.

  • google/diffusiongemma-26B-A4B-it — google | 1,010 likes, 601,208 downloads
    A breakthrough "diffusion transformer" that natively generates images and text, merging diffusion and autoregressive paradigms into a single instruct-tuned model.

  • moonshotai/Kimi-K2.7-Code — moonshotai | 909 likes, 274,865 downloads
    MoonShot's code-specialized multimodal model leveraging compressed-tensors for efficient image-to-code reasoning pipelines.

  • nvidia/LocateAnything-3B — nvidia | 2,194 likes, 228,669 downloads
    NVIDIA's lightweight universal object localization model: given an image and text prompt, it precisely locates any described entity.

3. Ecosystem Signal

MoE architectures are now the default. Nearly every major new model this week—DeepSeek-V4-Pro, GLM-5.2, MiniMax-M3, diffusiongemma, North-Mini-Code—uses mixture-of-experts. The era of dense 70B+ models dominating HuggingFace may be waning in favor of MoE models with 25-40B total parameters and 3-5B active parameters, which offer GPT-4-class intelligence at a fraction of inference cost.

Qwen derivatives dominate community fine-tuning. The Qwen3.6 and Qwen3.5-MoE families underpin roughly a third of this week's trending models, suggesting these base models have become the preferred bedrock for community customization—especially for uncensored, aggressive, or "personality" fine-tunes. This mirrors the earlier Llama-era pattern where one base model family achieved critical mass.

The GGUF ecosystem has become indispensable. Unsloth alone appears five times in the top 30, and GGUF variants now regularly out-download their original safetensors counterparts. The availability of high-quality quantization (from FP8 down to 2-bit) is democratizing access to models that would otherwise require enterprise GPU clusters. The diffusiongemma GGUF reaching 200K+ downloads in its first week signals that multimodal models are now firmly part of the local deployment ecosystem.

Government and enterprise open-weight releases. Rio-3.5-Open-397B from a city government and FastContext from Microsoft point to a maturing trend: institutional actors are releasing serious models openly, albeit often with caveats like restricted commercial use or geographical limitations.

4. Worth Exploring

  1. google/diffusiongemma-26B-A4B-it — This is arguably the most architecturally innovative model this week, merging diffusion and autoregressive generation in a single instruct-tuned MoE. Researchers and practitioners interested in the future of multimodal foundation models should study this release closely—it represents a potential paradigm shift away from separate diffusion and language models.

  2. nvidia/LocateAnything-3B — With 2,194 likes and immediate community adoption, this 3B model punches far above its weight. It enables precise visual grounding without requiring bounding box annotations, making it a powerful building block for vision-based agents, robotics, and retrieval-augmented multimodal pipelines. Its small footprint means it can run on device alongside other models.

  3. zai-org/GLM-5.2 — The DSA (Dual Sparse Attention) architecture in GLM-5.2 deserves attention for its potential to extend context windows without quadratic compute scaling. Combined with the FP8 quantization variant available, this release provides a ready-to-use testbed for exploring sparse attention mechanisms in production conversational AI systems.
